# trade-data-generator

> The universal market data layer for trading UIs.
> Simulation for development. Real exchange data for production.
> Same API. Same format. One library.

## What it is

trade-data-generator is a Node.js npm library that generates realistic market data for trading UIs. It supports price ticks, order book depth and OHLCV candlestick data for equity, forex and crypto markets.

The library works in two modes:
- Simulation mode — generates realistic fake data locally, no API keys, no network calls, works offline
- Live mode — connects to real exchange WebSocket streams (Binance built-in), no API key required for public data

Developers switch between modes with one config change. The events, data format and API are identical regardless of source.

## Why it exists

Building a trading UI requires live market data at every stage. Real data feeds cost money. Free APIs have rate limits. Hardcoded data looks broken on charts. trade-data-generator solves the development and production problem in one library.

## Key features

- Zero dependencies — nothing extra installs
- EventEmitter API — wire into any WebSocket server (Socket.io, ws, Pusher)
- Realistic price simulation — mean reversion, volatility, trend bias, Box-Muller distribution
- Accurate order book — bid always below ask, volume tapers with depth
- OHLCV candles — multiple intervals simultaneously (1s, 1m, 5m, 15m, 1h, 4h, 1d)
- Market hours — equity and forex respect open/close times via IANA timezones
- Binance connector — real-time data from Binance public WebSocket, no API key
- Pluggable connectors — build your own connector for any exchange
- TypeScript support — full .d.ts definitions included
- 167 tests — 91.9% coverage
- MIT license — free forever

## Supported market types

- crypto — always open, no market hours needed
- equity — respects market hours (e.g. NYSE 09:30-16:00 America/New_York)
- forex — respects market hours (e.g. UTC 00:00-23:59 weekdays)

## Available sources

- simulation (default) — fake realistic data, zero API keys, works offline
- binance — real Binance WebSocket data, no API key needed
- custom — extend BaseConnector to add any exchange

## Install

```bash
npm install trade-data-generator
```

## Quick start — simulation

```javascript
const { MarketFeed } = require('trade-data-generator')

const feed = new MarketFeed({
  source: 'simulation',
  type:   'crypto',
  pairs:  [
    { symbol: 'BTC/USDT', startPrice: 45000, volatility: 0.004 },
    { symbol: 'ETH/USDT', startPrice: 2800,  volatility: 0.005 },
  ]
})

feed.on('tick',   (data) => console.log(data.symbol, data.price))
feed.on('candle', (data) => console.log(data.interval, data.close))
feed.on('depth',  (data) => console.log(data.bids[0], data.asks[0]))

feed.start()
```

## Quick start — real Binance data

```javascript
const { MarketFeed } = require('trade-data-generator')

const feed = new MarketFeed({
  source: 'binance',   // one line change from simulation
  type:   'crypto',
  pairs:  [
    { symbol: 'BTC/USDT' },
    { symbol: 'ETH/USDT' },
  ]
})

feed.on('tick',   (data) => console.log(data.symbol, data.price, data.source))
feed.on('candle', (data) => console.log(data.interval, data.close))
feed.on('depth',  (data) => console.log(data.bids[0], data.asks[0]))

feed.start()
```

## Tick event data shape

```javascript
{
  symbol:    'BTC/USDT',
  type:      'crypto',
  source:    'binance',       // or 'simulation'
  timestamp:  1714900000000,
  price:      80920.00,
  bid:        80920.00,
  ask:        80920.01,
  spread:     0.01,
  volume:     14376.18,
  change:     +88.68,
  changePct:  +0.11,
  high24h:    82479.32,
  low24h:     80279.77,
  open24h:    80831.32,
  previous:   80919.99,
}
```

## Candle event data shape

```javascript
{
  symbol:    'BTC/USDT',
  interval:  '1m',
  openTime:  1714900000000,
  closeTime: 1714900060000,
  open:      80900.00,
  high:      80950.00,
  low:       80880.00,
  close:     80920.00,
  volume:    42.18,
  closed:    true,
  source:    'binance',
}
```

## WebSocket integration example

```javascript
const { MarketFeed } = require('trade-data-generator')
const { Server }     = require('socket.io')
const http           = require('http')

const server = http.createServer()
const io     = new Server(server)

const feed = new MarketFeed({
  source: 'binance',
  type:   'crypto',
  pairs:  [{ symbol: 'BTC/USDT' }]
})

feed.on('tick',   (data) => io.to(data.symbol).emit('ticker', data))
feed.on('candle', (data) => io.to(data.symbol).emit('candle', data))
feed.on('depth',  (data) => io.to(data.symbol).emit('book',   data))

io.on('connection', (socket) => {
  socket.on('subscribe', ({ symbol }) => {
    socket.join(symbol)
  })
})

feed.start()
server.listen(3001)
```

## Custom connector

```javascript
const { MarketFeed, BaseConnector, registerConnector } = require('trade-data-generator')

class MyExchangeConnector extends BaseConnector {
  async connect()            { /* open WebSocket */ }
  async disconnect()         { /* close WebSocket */ }
  async subscribe(symbols)   { /* subscribe to pairs */ }
  async unsubscribe(symbols) { /* unsubscribe */ }
  normalizeTick(raw)         { return { ...raw, source: 'myexchange' } }
  normalizeCandle(raw)       { return { ...raw, source: 'myexchange' } }
  normalizeDepth(raw)        { return { ...raw, source: 'myexchange' } }
}

registerConnector('myexchange', MyExchangeConnector)

const feed = new MarketFeed({
  source: 'myexchange',
  type:   'crypto',
  pairs:  [{ symbol: 'BTC/USDT' }]
})
```

## API methods

- feed.start() — start the feed
- feed.stop() — stop completely
- feed.pause() — pause ticks (simulation only)
- feed.resume() — resume after pause
- feed.getState(symbol) — current state for a symbol
- feed.getCandles(symbol, interval, limit) — candle history
- feed.getSymbols() — all symbol names
- feed.getSource() — active source name
- feed.getMarketStatus() — market open/close status
- feed.reset(symbol) — reset symbol to start price (simulation)
- feed.resetAll() — reset all symbols (simulation)
- feed.isRunning() — boolean
